author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Lust and Travail Come from Sin. Whence Our Members Became a Cause of Shame.
title: Lust and Travail Come from Sin. Whence Our Members Became a Cause of Shame.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 41 [XXXVI.]—Lust and Travail Come from Sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Whence Our Members Became a Cause of Shame.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Granted, therefore, that we have no means of showing both that the nuptial acts of that primeval marriage were quietly discharged, undisturbed by lustful passion, and that the motion of the organs of generation, like that of any other members of the body, was not instigated by the ardour of lust, but directed by the choice of the will (which would have continued such with marriage had not the disgrace of sin intervened); still, from all that is stated in the sacred Scriptures on divine authority, we have reasonable grounds for believing that such was the original condition of wedded life.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Although, it is true, I am not told that the nuptial embrace was unattended with prurient desire; as also I do not find it on record that parturition was unaccompanied with groans and pain, or that actual birth led not to future death; yet, at the same time, if I follow the verity of the Holy Scriptures, the travail of the mother and the death of the human offspring would never have supervened if sin had not preceded.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Nor would that have happened which abashed the man and woman when they covered their loins; because in the same sacred records it is expressly written that the sin was first committed, and then immediately followed this hiding of their shame.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For unless some indelicacy of motion had announced to their eyes—which were of course not closed, though not open to this point, that is, not attentive—that those particular members should be corrected, they would not have perceived anything on their own persons, which God had entirely made worthy of all praise, that called for either shame or concealment.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: If, indeed, the sin had not first occurred which they had dared to commit in their disobedience, there would not have followed the disgrace which their shame would fain conceal.
